description: The explicit `asIs` escape-hatch type does not fire E-TYPE-ANY-FORBIDDEN (§14.x). `a: asIs` is the sanctioned opaque type, so the any-token check stays silent. The clean twin of e-type-any-forbidden-pos.
rationale: §14.x `asIs` is the explicit, sanctioned opaque type; E-TYPE-ANY-FORBIDDEN fires only on the literal `any` token, which `asIs` replaces.
